item.title: This Week at Remap
item.content:encoded:
I cannot believe I recently purchased an Xbox controller specifically to make playing games via my Steam Deck on my TV better, and now Valve has announced a new Steam Controller that's coming in the next few weeks. (No, I haven't had a chance to try one yet!) Will I be buying one? Probably. What am I supposed to do with that new Xbox controller? I'm not sure. Did I already toss the box out, meaning I cannot return the controller? Pretty sure. Is the red color on that controller really nice? It sure is. Son of a bitch.

-pk, who, nonetheless, is still patiently waiting for a Steam Machine

  • SILKSONG SPOILERCAST: We're recording this week. You can submit questions, observations, and topics for us to discuss right over here.
  • PODCAST: My Turn – Logan Lucky (feat: Chia, Janet, Patrick, Rob) – What a joy this movie is! We go over this in the podcast, but for the life of me, I cannot remember why I was so jazzed to see this movie when it came out. But it was also a joy to revisit it here, and for some people, like Chia, to see it for the first time. Plus, I guess it means I'm finally going to watch the Ocean's movies.
  • STREAM: Baby Steps – Please, release me. Please, let me finish this thing.
  • ARTICLE/PODCAST: Titanium Court – My time is tight this week—mostly because I want to deliver the piece before Rob would have to hop onto a plane—but my plan is to turn around a great interview I had on Friday morning.
  • PODCAST: Remap Radio – No Rob this week; he'll be F1'ing it in Miami. We should be doing some catchup this week. Maybe Janet has finished Pragmata and we can do a mini spoiler cast? I'll have spent more time in Titanium Court and Replaced. Chia said something about a Pokémon game? Stay tuned.
  • STREAM: UNTITLED PLATFORMER PROJECT (feat: Janet, Patrick) – So, we've been kicking this idea around for a minute, but as part of a combinations of podcasts and streams, Janet and I are going to embark on a grand project: ranking every video game platformer that's ever existed, now and in the future. Come join us for the launch, yeah? (No, we haven't nailed down a name for it.)
    • Stream first. Podcast second. (Soon!) The structure of the stream will be Janet and I trying homework assignments from a personal "best 10 platformers ever," list in which we ask the other person to play games that aren't on our list.
  • ARTICLE: Letter Series – Topic TBD but I really enjoyed riffing on an idea that someone had sent in, so if you want to suggest a topic for Rob and I, please send it to questions@remapradio.com with the title "Letter Series."

If you have any support issues, send details to support@remapradio.com

Patrick Klepek (he/him) is an editor at Remap. In another life, he worked on horror movie sets, but instead, he also runs Crossplay, a newsletter about parenting and video games. You can follow him on Twitter, Threads, Mastodon, and Bluesky.

item.title: Last Oasis Has Opened a Public Test Realm for Season 6, Chapter 2, With a Wide-Ranging List of Changes
item.content:encoded:
The Last Oasis team has opened up their public test realm for Season 6, Chapter 2. The PTR features a number of changes and fixes that they are planning for season 2, which are pretty wide-ranging and include performance improvements and crash fixes, balance tweaks, adjustments to walkers and modules, and crafting reworks.

There are a number of performance improvements,  including performance optimizations,  as well as fixes for a number of issues.  Some of these performance improvements involve visual changes, like replacing volcano lava pools with a default lava plane, despawning obsidian from eruptions, and even adjusting some POIs to spawn more dynamically instead of just static placement.

Resources and crafting get a number of reworks, as well as player experience improvements. Some tools and resources are getting moved into different categories. Lava weapons are with the crafting bench. And they're also putting in checkboxes that you can use to allow containers as your source for remote crafting.

Central to Last Oasis' combat are the walkers and modules. Here,  they’ve improved  quarries and rebalanced their output, enabled walker modules on Balang and Silur, As well as made a couple of tweaks to cool Downs and durability. 

Overall, this is a pretty sizable update and it makes sense since it is going to be for the launch of a brand new season.

The PTR is available now, and if you are looking for details on what's included as well as how to join the test, you can check out the PTR announcement and patch notes. Now that it has launched, you can jump in to help test, with 10x XP gain and 5x resources.  



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/last-oasis-has-opened-a-public-test-realm-for-season-6-chapter-2-with-a-wide-ranging-list-of-changes-2000137916
